context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements the Shelly Duo GU10 LAN HTTP API contract in Kotlin using Ktor, covering endpoint paths, query parameter names, HTTP method, off semantics, status probing, and clean shutdown behavior.

checklist.name: Ktor CIO engine
checklist.description: Uses HttpClient(CIO) — the Ktor CIO engine — not OkHttp, Apache, or another engine

checklist.name: Plain HTTP
checklist.description: Base URL uses http:// (not https://) when constructing requests to the bulb

checklist.name: Color endpoint path
checklist.description: Color commands use /color/0 as the request path (not /light/0, /rgb, or any other path)

checklist.name: Color query parameters
checklist.description: Color calls include all of: turn, red, green, blue, and gain as query parameters

checklist.name: White endpoint path
checklist.description: White/temperature commands use /white/0 as the request path

checklist.name: White query parameters
checklist.description: White/temperature calls include all of: turn, temp, and brightness as query parameters

checklist.name: Off via turn=off
checklist.description: Turning the bulb off uses the turn=off parameter — does NOT set red=0, green=0, blue=0 to achieve off

checklist.name: Status endpoint
checklist.description: Reachability / health probe uses /status endpoint (not /ping or /health or another path)

checklist.name: Reachability timeout
checklist.description: The isReachable or equivalent function applies a timeout (e.g., withTimeout or similar) to the status probe

checklist.name: Shutdown hook
checklist.description: Registers a JVM shutdown hook (Runtime.getRuntime().addShutdownHook or equivalent) that turns the bulb off when the process exits

checklist.name: HTTP GET method
checklist.description: All API calls (color, white, off, status) use HTTP GET, not POST or PUT
